definition: Fluent or persuasive speaking or writing; the power to express ideas with clarity and grace.
example: The lawyer's eloquence moved the jury deeply.

definition: To continue doing something despite difficulty or delay in achieving success.
example: She persevered through years of setbacks before finally publishing her novel.

definition: Having mixed feelings or contradictory ideas about something or someone.
example: She felt ambivalent about leaving her hometown for the big city.

definition: At high speed; quickly and without delay.
example: The emergency team responded swiftly to the distress call.

definition: Not the one nor the other of two people or things.
example: Neither of the candidates won a majority in the first round.
We did not have sufficient time to review all the documents.
I am writing regarding your recent inquiry about the position.

definition: In contrast or comparison with the fact that; while on the contrary.
example: Some people prefer coffee in the morning, whereas others start with tea.

definition: Used to emphasize a statement or to agree with something just said.
example: "Was the performance good?" — "Indeed, it was outstanding."

definition: And other similar things; and so forth — used at the end of a list to indicate that further similar items are included.
example: The conference covered topics like AI, data science, et cetera.
